name: Los Venganza Del Alamo Chili
recipeIngredient: 2 pounds Pork, cubed (thick butterfly pork chops)
2 pounds Chuck beef, cut into cubes
1/2 cup Wesson oil or kidney suet
1 Tablespoon Mole (powdered), also called mole poblano
2 tablespoons Coriander seed (from Chinese parsley, cilantro)
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large pot, combine paprika, oregano, MSG, chili powder, cumin, beef bouillon, beer, and 2 cups water. Let simmer.
2. In a separate skillet, brown meat in batches using 1 pound or 1 1/2 pounds of Wesson oil or suet. Drain excess fat and add to the simmering spices. Continue cooking until all the meat is done.
3. Sauté chopped onion and garlic in 1 tablespoon oil or suet. Add the mixture to the spices and meat, adjusting water as needed. Simmer for 2 hours.
4. Stir the mole, sugar, coriander seed, hot sauce, and tomato sauce into the chili. Continue simmering for 45 minutes.
5. Dissolve masa harina flour in warm water to form a paste. Add it to the chili and season with salt to taste. Simmer for an additional 30 minutes.
6. For a hotter flavor, add more Louisiana Red Hot Sauce as desired. Serve hot from one pot.
